trigger_reason: R735 is rank 1 by mechanical-PASS proximity in E30 (kw=2, tree-stream max sim=0.40, q-rubric=0.38). Per program_v10.md §2.5 (preserved in v11), top-3 rounds receive full step-13 spec.
candidate_stripped: Attention-head pruning via nuclear-norm + 2nd/3rd cumulant traces of per-head attention matrices; rank by joint quantile of (Tr|A|, Tr(A A^*), Tr(A A^*)^2) and prune lowest 30%.

A_candidate.name: Trace-class multi-moment prune (R735)
A_candidate.description: Per attention head h, compute nuclear-norm Tr|A_h|, 2nd-moment Tr(A_h A_h^*), 3rd-moment Tr((A_h A_h^*)^{3/2}). Stack into 3-vector; rank heads by joint quantile (geometric mean of per-moment ranks). Prune heads in the bottom 30% of joint ranks.
A_candidate.distinctive_parameter: Multi-moment vector (3 cumulant traces) ranking, vs single-moment (magnitude or entropy) ranking
A_candidate.expected_behavior: If higher cumulants capture head-importance information that single-moment scores miss, A retains accuracy at higher prune ratios than B/C.

B_control.name: Single-moment nuclear-norm prune
B_control.description: Per head, compute only the nuclear-norm Tr|A_h|. Rank heads by nuclear-norm. Prune lowest 30%. Same training setup as A.
B_control.distinctive_parameter_value: Single moment (k=1) only; no higher cumulants
B_control.expected_behavior: Null hypothesis: nuclear-norm alone is sufficient; higher moments add no information at this scale.

C_baseline.name: Voita 2019 attention-entropy prune
C_baseline.description: Per head, compute mean attention entropy across training tokens. Rank by entropy (lower entropy = more focused = more important). Prune lowest 30%.
C_baseline.expected_behavior: Published baseline; expected to match or beat magnitude-based methods.

metric.name: SST-2 dev accuracy at 30% head-prune ratio (post-fine-tuning, 3 epochs)
metric.measurement_protocol: Train BERT-base for 3 epochs on SST-2; compute per-head importance score per variant; prune 30%; re-evaluate on dev. Repeat with 3 seeds (42, 137, 271).

statistical_test.name: Paired t-test A vs B + paired t-test A vs C
statistical_test.expected_outcome_if_candidate_has_value: A significantly outperforms B (higher moments add information) AND not significantly worse than C.
statistical_test.expected_outcome_if_candidate_collapses_: A indistinguishable from B (joint quantile dominated by 1st moment; higher moments add noise not signal at this scale).

distinguishability_pre_check.rationale: Higher cumulant traces ARE structurally distinct from nuclear norm — they capture skewness/kurtosis of the spectrum. For attention matrices with heavy-tailed singular-value distributions (common in well-trained transformers), 2nd and 3rd moments DO carry information beyond the 1st. However, at 12 heads × 12 layers = 144 heads, the joint-quantile ranking may be dominated by the 1st moment due to range compression; if so, A reduces to B. Worth a pilot.
distinguishability_pre_check.implication: Worth running. If A ≈ B, R735 is a parametrization of nuclear-norm pruning (R279-pattern); if A > B by statistically significant margin, trace-class multi-moment is a real signal.

notes_for_executor: Use bert-base-uncased. For higher moments use torch.linalg.matrix_norm(A,'nuc') for 1st and (A@A.T) eigenvalue cumulants for 2nd/3rd.
